_README: Editable narrative + Monday-sourced bits for the weekly V2 report. The tracker Excel drives estate/board/journey; Linear drives tickets. Everything here is hand-maintained: update the snapshot, net-new, push, decisions, platform issues, and the journey history/labels each week, then run npm run build:v2-week. ticketFallback is used only if the Linear/Supabase read fails.
lede: We've moved most of the estate to the doorstep of done: 38 of 46 migrations are at or near the finish line, the entire Wipro FSS cluster enters customer UAT this week, and blocker inflow has finally caught up with burn-down. What's left is a short, known list — a handful of engineering blockers (browser automation, Mitie's UK instance, a few connections) between here and finishing the migration.
snapshotNote: Two sources. Live-in-production, footprint, and enhancements are the delivery portfolio estate (migration tracker + delivered history) — stable week to week. In-active-development and queued are the live Monday Projects board. The migration program's own stage board is below.
netNewDelta: Live Monday pull. vs prior: TTX Property Tax advanced to UAT; JBI Receiving moved to Waiting (VPN access); Conectiv SONY in discovery.

netNew.update: Completed in V2; waiting on customer QA support.
Browser-automation fixes landed; dev complete but unstable in live end-to-end — testing before customer UAT.
UAT + parity testing started; go-live on customer sign-off.
Building against the live system; awaiting VPN access.
Skeleton built; awaiting customer data for end-to-end.
Discovery call 1 done; call 2 held Jul 10.
Queued behind Import; no update posted yet.

boardDelta: Staged from tracker handover / parity / validation dates, not just the status field. Mitie PCard is blocked (needs v2 UK instance) — add its blocker to the Excel so it classifies correctly.
boardFootnote: Stage is derived from the tracker's parity-test, customer-handover and validation dates — not just the status field, which lags: several processes still marked 'engg pending' or 'parity' have customer-handover dates this week. Blocked = open engineering blocker with no customer date yet (from the Excel Blockers column). Earlier weeks were staged from Monday status, so part of the step up is this fuller date-based measure. These are the migration program (of the tracked V1 processes).
pushTitle: This week's push · field team (SE · FDE · CSM)

push.title: Move UAT to live
push.body: Processes in customer UAT: drive sign-offs. Wipro FSS cluster enters UAT this week (max 5 workstreams per customer). Plunkett pends their NetSuite fix; TTX needs collections + native email.

push.title: Start customer UAT from parity
push.body: Processes in parity: JBI PIR v2 queued; Wipro cluster validated vs V1 runs, scale answer feeds the subprocess benchmark.

push.title: Unblock engineering
push.body: Kort browser validation, Mitie v2 UK instance, Century collections fuzzy-match, Ciena base64, Conectiv large-file handling are the active blockers.

push.title: Keep builds and live processes healthy
push.body: Net-new V2 builds active; support live V1 and V2 production processes — open platform issues below.

_platformIssuesNote: V1 + platform production incidents outside the migration label. Verify open/closed against live Linear each week (open Urgent/High, not in_scope-limited). Phase 2: auto-derive from the open_urgent_high sweep.
platformIssuesTitle: Live production issues needing attention · V1 + platform (outside the migration label)

platformIssues.title: Conectiv · V1 — SharePoint download renames files, breaks downstream segregation
Indium · V1 — GPEH skips Gemini extraction, wrong enrollment field values
Wipro · V1 — DynamoDB lock loses brain context on large PDFs, run fails
Pepsico · V1 — ServiceNow tagging process crashing
Platform — OOMKills on browser-pool pods in prod
platformIssues.note: same infra behind Kort / Century browser work

ticketTrend.intro: Two views of the tracked v2 Linear set: the open backlog right now, and how new tickets have flowed in. Severity labeling (hard blocker / workaround / bug) began mid-June, so the 90-day inflow split shows as not tracked, not zero.
ticketTrend.note: Open now = tickets still open regardless of when filed (current backlog, classified today); the windows are new tickets by created date (inflow). Not tracked ≠ zero: severity wasn't labeled before mid-June. Created / resolved / open use creation and completion dates and are reliable across the span. Bug is inflated because ux-quality and Voyager feedback map to Bug by convention; cancelled / duplicate excluded from class columns.

ticketsFootnote: The velocity table above is the tracked-set view; the open list is the current migration backlog grouped by domain. Counts are live from Linear.

decisions.title: 1 · Subprocess and parallel execution
decisions.body: Building sequential and benchmarking runtime against V1. HTTP-draft workaround exists, not in use. Alternative: parallelize inside Books — parallel IDP shipped, parallel file processing next candidate.
decisions.decide: Prioritize native subprocess based on how many processes stall or run materially slower than V1.

decisions.title: 2 · V1 Python and integration code
decisions.body: Full rebuild in SPy is viable for BCI procedures; Coupa and Maximo can run over direct HTTP calls for testing.
decisions.decide: Keep as-is through go-live and handover, or package as a product feature.

decisions.title: 3 · UK instance for Mitie
decisions.body: Mitie's V1 processes run in the UK instance; V2 has none. Testing proceeds on app.us-1.
decisions.decide: Stand up a UK V2 instance or approve an alternative region — gates the account.

sources: Sources: migration tracker (refreshed on upload) drives the estate and stage board; the live Monday Projects board drives net-new and active/queued; Linear (live) drives the blocker burnup, velocity table, open backlog, and ticket groups. Severity labeling began mid-June, so the 90-day class split is shown as not tracked. Live-production and footprint totals are the delivery portfolio estate. Push lanes, decisions, and the platform-issues list are hand-maintained in v2-week-narrative.json.

openNow.asOf: Still open, from the migration-tracked Linear tickets of the last 90 days. Current backlog, not the recent inflow below.
